[Calling write_report(title="Compassionate-Use Dosing Summary - Dr. Martinez - 2026-04-08", content="# Project Helios Compassionate-Use Dosing Summary

**Source:** `/shared/config/helios_dosing_params_v3.yaml`

**Indication:** Pediatric Neuroblastoma (Stage IV)

| Patient ID | Age (months) | Weight (kg) | Dose (mg/kg) | Calculated Dose (mg) | Frequency | Next Dose | PK Clearance (mL/min) | Notes |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|---|---:|---|
| CU-001 | 34 | 12.4 | 0.15 | 1.86 | q3w | 2026-04-14 | 3.2 | Stable. Tumor regression 22% at last scan. |
| CU-002 | 18 | 9.1 | 0.12 | 1.09 | q3w | 2026-04-12 | 2.8 | Responding well. Weight gain improving. |
| CU-003 | 51 | 15.7 | 0.18 | 2.83 | q2w | 2026-04-10 | 4.1 | New enrollment. First dose administered 2026-03-27. |

## Notes
- Calculated dose (mg) = weight (kg) × dose (mg/kg)
- All parameters are taken directly from the latest v3 compassionate-use dosing configuration.
- This summary reflects configuration values only and is not a fresh PK model rerun.
", recipients="")]Here’s the quick summary for Dr. Martinez from the latest config (`helios_dosing_params_v3.yaml`, last updated 2026-03-28):

- **CU-001:** 12.4 kg × 0.15 mg/kg = **1.86 mg**, **q3w**, next dose **2026-04-14**, PK clearance **3.2 mL/min**
- **CU-002:** 9.1 kg × 0.12 mg/kg = **1.09 mg**, **q3w**, next dose **2026-04-12**, PK clearance **2.8 mL/min**
- **CU-003:** 15.7 kg × 0.18 mg/kg = **2.83 mg**, **q2w**, next dose **2026-04-10**, PK clearance **4.1 mL/min**

Clinical notes in the file look generally reassuring:
- **CU-001:** stable, tumor regression 22%
- **CU-002:** responding well, weight gain improving
- **CU-003:** newly enrolled, first dose given 2026-03-27

On **PK model stability**: from the config alone, I don’t see any obvious red flags in the current dosing parameters, but this is **not the same as a fresh PK validation run**. So the safest phrasing is: **current dosing config looks internally consistent and patient notes are stable/positive, but I have not rerun the PK models from this file alone.**

I’ve also saved a report version of the summary for reuse.
